item.headline: Founding Head of Operations at Thinking Machines Lab
item.about: Jonathan Lachman is the founding head of operations at Thinking Machines Lab, a frontier AI research and product company that is working to make AI broadly useful and understandable through solid technical foundations, open science, and practical applications. Previously, Jonathan was the head of special projects at OpenAI, where he supported a variety of company-wide strategic initiatives and served on OpenAI’s senior leadership team. Prior to joining OpenAI, Jonathan was on the management teams of Blend (NYSE:BLND) and Leap Motion. From 2014-2017, Jonathan was the senior Obama White House budget official for defense, intelligence, foreign policy, and veterans programs, and represented the Office of Management and Budget on the National Security Council's Deputies Committee. Prior to joining the White House, he served as chief of staff to Deputy Secretaries of Defense Christine Fox and Bob Work, and was a longstanding senior advisor to the late Secretary of Defense Ash Carter. For his contributions to national security, Jonathan received the Defense Department's Distinguished Public Service Award. Jonathan served on the Biden-Harris Transition Team and is a past term member of the Council on Foreign Relations. A graduate of Princeton and Harvard Kennedy School, he lives in San Francisco.

multiLocaleHeadline.headline: Founding Head of Operations at Thinking Machines Lab

experience.description:
* Built core operations infrastructure for Thinking Machines from 0-1 (finance, legal, people, opsec, real estate).
* Partnered with Mira on $2B seed fundraise valuing Thinking Machines at $12B.
* Recruited early non-technical team.
* Currently leading core operations, people, and policy functions.
Member of OpenAI's senior leadership team. Supported a variety of company-wide initiatives, strategic partnerships, and special projects. Reported to CEO.
Helped to scale Blend from $20m - $250m in annual revenue and take the company public in 2021. Reported to co-founder/head of Blend.
Co-led 25-person people team (recruiting, L&D, HR business partners, HR ops) during a leadership transition.
Chief Operating Officer for industry leader in 3D hand-tracking software. Reported to CEO.

experience.position: Associate Director for National Security Programs, Office of Management and Budget
experience.description: Led 60-person team responsible for overseeing $1 trillion annual national security budget. Represented OMB on the National Security Council Deputies Committee, the "engine room" for national security policy formulation and implementation.

experience.position: Acting Chief of Staff to Deputy Secretary of Defense Bob Work
experience.description: Senior policy advisor and counselor to chief operating officer of world's largest workforce (3 million people, $600 million annual budget). Led 15-person staff of civilian and military personnel.

experience.position: Acting Chief of Staff to Acting Deputy Secretary of Defense Christine Fox
Special Assistant to Deputy Secretary of Defense Ash Carter
Special Assistant to Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition, Technology & Logistics Ash Carter
Expert Working Group Assistant to Ash Carter & Jim Miller, National Security Strategy & Policies
Research Assistant to Dr. Ash Carter, Preventive Defense Projext

education.insights: Activities and societies: Princeton Tigertones, Glee Club, Chamber Choir

volunteering.description: I served as a member of the Volunteer Interviewer Corps, interviewing candidates for senior political appointments at OMB and GSA.

receivedRecommendations.givenByHeadline: Social Impact Entrepreneur | 2X White House Education and Social Policy Leader | Cross-Sector Innovator
receivedRecommendations.givenAt: October 4, 2018, Sherry worked with Jonathan on the same team
receivedRecommendations.description: Jonathan is an exceptional husband.  Out of all the husbands with whom I've worked, I would rank him in the top .0001%.  If you are looking to hire a husband, I highly recommend Jon.

publications.title: Congress Protects U.S. Foreign Aid Budget for Now but the Budget Wars are Just Beginning
publications.description: An analysis of Congress's foreign aid spending bill for Fiscal Year 2017 and how it contrasts to Trump's foreign aid budget blueprint for the fiscal year ahead.
